docs.title: iHela SDK
docs.text:
Python SDK for the iHela Credit Union API — a financial services platform for banking, payments, and mobile money in Burundi.

This library provides typed, authenticated access to all iHela services with both synchronous and asynchronous clients backed by httpx.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• OAuth2 Authentication — client_credentials and authorization_code grant flows with automatic token refresh. Inject pre-obtained tokens or defer auth.
  • Merchant Services — Initiate and verify bills, cash-in to customer accounts, query bank lists, and perform customer lookups.
  • Banking Services — Deposits, withdrawals, account lookups, balance checks, mini-statements, transaction status, and fee calculation.
  • Agent Services — Agent deposits, withdrawal validation, and operation lookups.
  • Framework-Agnostic — No web framework dependency. OAuth2 primitives work with Django, Flask, FastAPI, or any Python application.
  • Async & Sync — Identical API surface via BankingClient / AsyncBankingClient, AgentClient / AsyncAgentClient, and MerchantClient / AsyncMerchantClient.
  • Type-Checked — PEP 561 py.typed marker and full type annotations.
  • Input Validation — Pydantic models validate deposit, withdrawal, and validation payloads before they reach the gateway.
  • Security — HMAC-SHA256 request signing, sensitive field masking in debug logs, credential-safe __repr__, explicit token clearing.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Getting Started — Install, authenticate, and make your first call.
  • API Reference — Complete reference for all clients, methods, and schemas.
  • Authentication — OAuth2 flows and framework integration (Django, Flask, FastAPI).

docs.title: Related Links
docs.text:
• iHela Official Documentation
  • GitHub Repository
  • PyPI Package

docs.title: MerchantClient
docs.text:
Handles payments, bills, bank lists, and customer lookups.

from ihela_sdk import MerchantClient

client = MerchantClient("client_id", "client_secret", prod=False)
Parameter Type Default Description client_id str required OAuth2 client ID from iHela client_secret str required OAuth2 client secret prod bool False True for production, False for test gateway ihela_url str None Override the base gateway URL

docs.title: customer_lookup(bank_slug, customer_id=None, account_number=None)
docs.text:
Look up a customer account by bank slug and account number or customer ID.

customer = client.customer_lookup(bank_slug="MF1-0001", account_number="76000111")
# {'account_number': '000001-01', 'name': 'Bigirimana Fabrice', 'response_status': 200}

docs.title: init_bill(amount, user, description, reference, *, bank=None, bank_client_id=None, redirect_uri=None, pin_code=None, merchant_description=None, payment_product_id=None)
docs.text:
Create a payment bill. The user must confirm payment in iHela.

Parameter Type Description amount int Amount in BIF user str Customer email, phone, or iHela ID description str Payment description reference str Unique merchant reference bank str \| None Bank slug (e.g. "MF1-0001") pin_code str \| None 4-digit pin redirect_uri str \| None Redirect URL after payment 
bill = client.init_bill(
    amount=600,
    user="76000111",
    description="Product Purchase",
    reference="merchant-ref-001",
    bank="MF1-0001",
    redirect_uri="https://yourapp.com/callback/",
    pin_code="1234",
)
# {'bill': {'code': 'CODE-20230321-9E29QH1', ...}, 'response_status': 200}

docs.title: verify_bill(bill_code, merchant_reference, pin_code)
docs.text:
Check if a bill has been paid, is pending, expired, or cancelled.

status = client.verify_bill(
    bill_code="CODE-20230321-9E29QH1",
    merchant_reference="merchant-ref-001",
    pin_code="1234",
)
# response_data.status can be: "Initial", "Paid", "Canceled", or "Error"

docs.title: cashin_client(bank_slug, account, amount, merchant_reference, description, *, pin_code=None, credit_account_holder=None, currency="BIF")
docs.text:
Send money or refund a customer account.

cashin = client.cashin_client(
    bank_slug="MF1-0001",
    account="000001-01",
    amount=20000,
    merchant_reference="REF3223",
    description="Refunding customer",
    pin_code="1234",
)

docs.title: get_bank_list()
docs.text:
Returns all available payment banks.

banks = client.get_bank_list()
# {'banks': [{'slug': 'MF1-0001', 'name': 'iHela Credit Union', ...}], ...}

docs.title: get_cashin_bank_list() / get_cashout_bank_list()
docs.text: Returns banks supporting cash-in or cash-out operations.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Method Description get_access_token() Returns the current access token is_authenticated() Whether the client has a valid token

docs.title: BankingClient
docs.text:
Banking operations: deposits, withdrawals, account management, and transactions.

from ihela_sdk import BankingClient

client = BankingClient("client_id", "client_secret", prod=False)
Parameter Type Default Description client_id str required OAuth2 client ID client_secret str required OAuth2 client secret prod bool False Environment selector ihela_url str None Override base URL ssl_cert any None SSL client certificate path signature_key str None HMAC signing key for X-iHela-Signature header

docs.title: ping()
docs.text:
Health check — verifies connectivity and authentication.

client.ping()
# {'success': True, 'response_status': 200}

docs.title: request_token(username, password)
docs.text:
Request tokens via username/password (alternative to client credentials).

tokens = client.request_token("username", "password")
# {'access': '...', 'refresh': '...'}

docs.title: account_lookup(account_number)
docs.text:
Retrieve account details by account number.

info = client.account_lookup("76077736")

docs.title: account_balance(account_number)
docs.text:
Get the current balance for an account.

balance = client.account_balance("76077736")

docs.title: deposit(credit_account, credit_account_holder, amount, description, external_reference, pin_code, external_code="")
docs.text:
Deposit funds into an account. Payload is validated with DepositPayload.

Parameter Type Description credit_account str Target account number credit_account_holder str Account holder name amount float Positive amount description str Transaction description external_reference str Your unique reference pin_code str 4-6 digit pin external_code str Optional external code 
client.deposit(
    credit_account="ACT-123",
    credit_account_holder="John Doe",
    amount=500.0,
    description="Invoice #42",
    external_reference="REF-001",
    pin_code="1234",
)

docs.title: withdrawal(debit_account, debit_account_holder, amount, description, external_reference, pin_code)
docs.text:
Withdraw funds from an account. Payload is validated with WithdrawalPayload.

client.withdrawal(
    debit_account="ACT-123",
    debit_account_holder="John Doe",
    amount=1000.0,
    description="Cash withdrawal",
    external_reference="REF-002",
    pin_code="1234",
)

docs.title: statement(account_number)
docs.text:
Get a mini-statement for an account.

stmt = client.statement("76077736")

docs.title: transaction_status(external_reference, reference)
docs.text:
Check the status of a transaction.

status = client.transaction_status("REF-001", "internal-reference")

docs.title: transaction_fee(currency, operation_type, amount)
docs.text:
Query transaction fees before performing an operation.

Parameter Type Description currency str ISO currency code (e.g. "BIF") operation_type str "deposit", "withdrawal", etc. amount str Amount to query 
fee = client.transaction_fee("BIF", "withdrawal", "4000")
# {'success': True, 'response_data': {'fee': '100', 'currency': 'BIF', ...}}

docs.title: Token Management
docs.text: Method Description authenticate() Acquire a new access token refresh_token() Refresh an expired access token is_authenticated() Check if token is valid

docs.title: AgentClient
docs.text:
Agent-specific operations: deposits and withdrawal validation.

from ihela_sdk import AgentClient

client = AgentClient("client_id", "client_secret", prod=False)

Same constructor parameters as BankingClient.

docs.title: ping()
docs.text: Health check.

docs.title: request_token(username, password)
docs.text: Username/password token request.

docs.title: account_lookup(account_number) / account_balance(account_number)
docs.text: Same as BankingClient.

docs.title: deposit(credit_account, credit_account_holder, amount, description, external_reference, pin_code, external_code="")
docs.text: Agent deposit. Validated with DepositPayload. Hits agent-deposit/ endpoint.

docs.title: operation_lookup(operation_code, amount)
docs.text:
Look up an operation by its code.

result = client.operation_lookup("op-code", "5000")
# {'validation_operation_code': 'val-123', ...}

docs.title: validate_withdrawal(external_reference, pin_code, agent_code, amount, external_code, validation_operation_code)
docs.text:
Validate and confirm a withdrawal. Validated with ValidateWithdrawalPayload.

client.validate_withdrawal(
    external_reference="REF-123",
    pin_code="1234",
    agent_code="AGT-001",
    amount="5000",
    external_code="EXT-001",
    validation_operation_code="VAL-001",
)

docs.title: transaction_status(external_reference, reference)
docs.text: Same as BankingClient.

docs.title: Async Clients
docs.text:
Every client has an async counterpart. Import from ihela_sdk:

Sync Async MerchantClient AsyncMerchantClient BankingClient AsyncBankingClient AgentClient AsyncAgentClient 
from ihela_sdk import AsyncBankingClient

async def main():
    client = AsyncBankingClient("id", "secret")
    await client.authenticate()
    balance = await client.account_balance("76077736")

All methods are identical — just await them.

docs.title: Pydantic Models
docs.text:
The SDK exports validation models you can use directly:

from ihela_sdk import DepositPayload, WithdrawalPayload, ValidateWithdrawalPayload

# Validate before calling the API
payload = DepositPayload(
    credit_account="ACT-1234",
    credit_account_holder="John Doe",
    amount=1500.50,
    description="Invoice Payment",
    external_reference="REF-883",
    pin_code="1234",
)

docs.title: DepositPayload
docs.text: Field Type Constraints credit_account str 3-50 chars, [A-Z0-9\-] credit_account_holder str 2-100 chars amount float > 0 description str 1-255 chars external_reference str 3-100 chars pin_code str 4-6 digits external_code str Optional, max 100 chars

docs.title: WithdrawalPayload
docs.text: Field Type Constraints debit_account str 3-50 chars, [A-Z0-9\-] debit_account_holder str 2-100 chars amount float > 0 description str 1-255 chars external_reference str 3-100 chars pin_code str 4-6 digits

docs.title: Sensitive Data Masking
docs.text:
All API responses are automatically scanned before debug logging. The following fields are replaced with ********:

pin_code, access_token, refresh_token, client_secret, client_id, password, token, secret, api_key, authorization

docs.title: Safe Object Representation
docs.text:
__repr__ never exposes credentials — it only shows the class name and environment flag:

>>> client
<BankingClient prod_env=False>

docs.title: Token Lifecycle
docs.text:
All clients support clear_token() to remove the access token from memory:

client.clear_token()  # auth_token_object is now None

docs.title: Token Injection
docs.text:
Pass a pre-obtained token to skip authentication — no credentials are transmitted:

client = BankingClient("id", "secret",
    token={"access_token": "...", "token_type": "Bearer"})

client = BankingClient("id", "secret", auto_auth=False)
# Authenticates lazily on the first API call

docs.title: Request Signing
docs.text:
Provide a signature_key to Banking or Agent clients to HMAC-SHA256 sign every deposit, withdrawal, and validation request:

client = BankingClient("id", "secret", signature_key="your-key")
client.deposit(...)
# Adds X-iHela-Signature: <hmac-sha256-hex> header

docs.title: Webhook Verification
docs.text:
Verify incoming webhook signatures from iHela:

from ihela_sdk import verify_signature

if verify_signature(payload, signature, secret_key):
    process_webhook(payload)

docs.title: Production Guardrails
docs.text:
All sync clients (BankingClient, AgentClient, MerchantClient, MerchantAuthorizationClient) support the following guardrails via constructor parameters.

Parameter Type Default Description rate_limit int 0 (disabled) Max requests per minute per instance circuit_breaker_threshold int 5 Consecutive failures before opening circuit circuit_breaker_cooldown float 30.0 Seconds before re-probing after open

docs.title: Circuit Breaker
docs.text:
client = BankingClient("id", "secret",
    circuit_breaker_threshold=3, circuit_breaker_cooldown=60.0)

try:
    client.ping()
except iHelaCircuitOpenError:
    # Circuit is open — wait before retrying

Check circuit state:

client.circuit_breaker.state    # "closed", "open", "half_open"
client.circuit_breaker.failure_count

docs.title: Rate Limiter
docs.text:
client = BankingClient("id", "secret", rate_limit=100)

try:
    client.deposit(...)
except iHelaRateLimitError:
    # Client-side rate limit exceeded

docs.title: Idempotency
docs.text:
On deposit() and withdrawal() operations, if external_reference is empty, a UUID is auto-generated to prevent duplicate transactions:

# Auto-generated external_reference
client.deposit(credit_account="ACT-123", credit_account_holder="John",
               amount=500.0, description="Payment", pin_code="1234")

# Explicit idempotency key for retries
client.deposit(credit_account="ACT-123", credit_account_holder="John",
               amount=500.0, description="Payment", pin_code="1234",
               external_reference="my-unique-id")

docs.title: Endpoint Reference
docs.text: The SDK maps to the following iHela API endpoints:

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Method Path Description POST oAuth2/token/ Obtain access token (client credentials) POST ihela/api/v1/auth-token/ Obtain token via username/password POST ihela/api/v1/auth-token/refresh/ Refresh access token

docs.title: Merchant Services
docs.text: Method Path Description GET api/v1/payments/bank/ List all banks GET api/v1/payments/bank/cashin/ List cash-in banks GET api/v1/payments/bank/cashout/ List cash-out banks GET api/v1/bank/{slug}/account/lookup/ Customer account lookup POST api/v1/payments/bill-init/ Initialize a bill POST api/v1/payments/bill-check/ Verify bill status POST api/v1/payments/cash-in/ Cash-in to customer account

docs.title: Banking Services
docs.text: Method Path Description GET ihela/api/v1/ping/ Health check POST ihela/api/v1/account-lookup/ Account lookup POST ihela/api/v1/bsces/balance/ Account balance POST ihela/api/v1/make-deposit/ Deposit funds POST ihela/api/v1/make-withdrawal/ Withdraw funds GET ihela/api/v1/mini-statement/ Mini statement POST ihela/api/v1/transaction-status/ Transaction status POST ihela/api/v1/transaction-fee/ Transaction fee query

docs.title: Agent Services
docs.text: Method Path Description GET ihela/api/v1/ping/ Health check POST ihela/api/v1/account-lookup/ Account lookup POST ihela/api/v1/bsces/balance/ Account balance POST ihela/api/v1/agent-deposit/ Agent deposit POST ihela/api/v1/operation-lookup/ Operation lookup POST ihela/api/v1/validate-withdrawal/ Validate withdrawal POST ihela/api/v1/transaction-status/ Transaction status

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: The SDK is framework-agnostic — it doesn't depend on Django, Flask, FastAPI, or any specific web framework. You manage routes, sessions, and token storage in your application, and the SDK provides the OAuth2 primitives.

docs.title: Client Credentials (Machine-to-Machine)
docs.text:
For backend services that authenticate without a user:

from ihela_sdk import BankingClient

client = BankingClient("your_client_id", "your_client_secret")
# Authenticates automatically on init

docs.title: Authorization Code (User-to-Machine / SSO)
docs.text: For web applications where a user signs in through iHela:

docs.title: 1. Redirect the user to iHela
docs.text:
from ihela_sdk import MerchantAuthorizationClient

auth = MerchantAuthorizationClient(
    "your_client_id", "your_client_secret",
    prod=False,  # True for production
)

login_url = auth.get_authorization_url(
    redirect_uri="https://yourapp.com/callback/"
)
# Redirect user to login_url

docs.title: 2. Handle the callback
docs.text:
# In your callback route handler:
code = request.GET.get("code")  # or request.args.get("code") in Flask

auth.authenticate(code, redirect_uri="https://yourapp.com/callback/")
user = auth.get_user_info()

# Store the token in your session:
session["ihela_token"] = {
    "access_token": auth.get_access_token(),
    "token_type": auth.get_token_type(),
}

docs.title: 3. Use the token on subsequent requests
docs.text:
client = BankingClient(
    "your_client_id", "your_client_secret",
    token=session["ihela_token"],
)
balance = client.account_balance("76077736")

docs.title: Django
docs.text:
# urls.py
urlpatterns = [
    path("ihela/login/", views.ihela_login, name="ihela-login"),
    path("ihela/callback/", views.ihela_callback, name="ihela-callback"),
]


# views.py
def ihela_login(request):
    auth = MerchantAuthorizationClient(
        settings.IHELA_CLIENT_ID, settings.IHELA_CLIENT_SECRET,
        prod=not settings.IHELA_TEST_ENV,
    )
    url = auth.get_authorization_url(
        redirect_uri=request.build_absolute_uri("/ihela/callback/")
    )
    return redirect(url)


def ihela_callback(request):
    code = request.GET["code"]
    auth = MerchantAuthorizationClient(
        settings.IHELA_CLIENT_ID, settings.IHELA_CLIENT_SECRET,
        prod=not settings.IHELA_TEST_ENV,
    )
    auth.authenticate(code, redirect_uri=request.build_absolute_uri("/ihela/callback/"))
    request.session["ihela_token"] = {
        "access_token": auth.get_access_token(),
        "token_type": auth.get_token_type(),
    }
    return redirect("/dashboard")

docs.title: Flask
docs.text:
@app.route("/ihela/login")
def ihela_login():
    auth = MerchantAuthorizationClient(
        app.config["IHELA_CLIENT_ID"], app.config["IHELA_CLIENT_SECRET"],
        prod=False,
    )
    url = auth.get_authorization_url(redirect_uri=url_for("ihela_callback", _external=True))
    return redirect(url)


@app.route("/ihela/callback")
def ihela_callback():
    code = request.args["code"]
    auth = MerchantAuthorizationClient(
        app.config["IHELA_CLIENT_ID"], app.config["IHELA_CLIENT_SECRET"],
        prod=False,
    )
    auth.authenticate(code, redirect_uri=url_for("ihela_callback", _external=True))
    session["ihela_token"] = {
        "access_token": auth.get_access_token(),
        "token_type": auth.get_token_type(),
    }
    return redirect("/dashboard")

docs.title: FastAPI
docs.text:
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse

@app.get("/ihela/login")
async def ihela_login(request: Request):
    auth = MerchantAuthorizationClient(
        settings.ihela_client_id, settings.ihela_client_secret, prod=False
    )
    url = auth.get_authorization_url(redirect_uri=str(request.url_for("ihela_callback")))
    return RedirectResponse(url)


@app.get("/ihela/callback")
async def ihela_callback(request: Request, code: str):
    auth = MerchantAuthorizationClient(
        settings.ihela_client_id, settings.ihela_client_secret, prod=False
    )
    auth.authenticate(code, redirect_uri=str(request.url_for("ihela_callback")))
    request.session["ihela_token"] = {
        "access_token": auth.get_access_token(),
        "token_type": auth.get_token_type(),
    }
    return RedirectResponse("/dashboard")

docs.title: Token Injection
docs.text:
Skip authentication entirely by passing a pre-obtained token — useful for load-balanced services or when the token is managed externally:

client = BankingClient("id", "secret",
    token={"access_token": "...", "token_type": "Bearer"})

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• Never commit credentials. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
  • Store tokens in your framework's session mechanism — the SDK does not persist tokens.
  • The MerchantAuthorizationClient is for user-facing OAuth2 flows only. Use BankingClient / AgentClient / MerchantClient for API operations.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install ihela-sdk

Or with uv:

uv add ihela-sdk

Requirements: Python 3.10+

Dependencies: httpx (HTTP client), pydantic (validation)

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All clients authenticate automatically using OAuth2 client credentials. Provide your client_id and client_secret obtained from iHela.

docs.title: Merchant Services
docs.text:
from ihela_sdk import MerchantClient

client = MerchantClient("your_client_id", "your_client_secret", prod=False)

docs.title: Banking Services
docs.text:
from ihela_sdk import BankingClient

client = BankingClient("your_client_id", "your_client_secret", prod=False)

docs.title: Agent Services
docs.text:
from ihela_sdk import AgentClient

client = AgentClient("your_client_id", "your_client_secret", prod=False)

Set prod=True for the production environment. The default (False) uses the test gateway at https://testgate.ihela.online/.

docs.title: Environment URLs
docs.text:
Environment Gateway URL Test https://testgate.ihela.online/ Production https://gate.ihela.online/

Override with a custom URL via the ihela_url parameter on any client.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
from ihela_sdk import MerchantClient

client = MerchantClient("your_client_id", "your_client_secret")
bill = client.init_bill(
    amount=2000,
    user="customer@example.com",
    description="Invoice #123",
    reference="merchant-ref-001",
    pin_code="1234",
)

status = client.verify_bill(
    bill_code=bill["bill"]["code"],
    merchant_reference=bill["bill"]["merchant_reference"],
    pin_code="1234",
)

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The SDK raises structured exceptions on failures:

from ihela_sdk import (
    iHelaError,
    iHelaAuthenticationError,
    iHelaAPIError,
)

try:
    result = client.ping()
except iHelaAuthenticationError:
    print("Invalid credentials or connection refused.")
except iHelaAPIError as e:
    print(f"Gateway error (HTTP {e.status_code})")
except iHelaError:
    print("General client error.")
  • iHelaAuthenticationError — Raised when authentication fails or the gateway is unreachable.
  • iHelaAPIError — Raised on invalid inputs or gateway errors. The message is intentionally generic to prevent information leakage.

docs.title: Credential Protection
docs.text:
The SDK never logs client credentials or access tokens. All sensitive fields (pin_code, access_token, client_secret, password, etc.) in API responses are automatically masked with ******** before debug logging.

Client objects have a safe __repr__ that only shows the environment flag:

>>> client
<BankingClient prod_env=False>   # no credentials exposed

docs.title: Token Injection
docs.text:
Skip authentication entirely by passing a pre-obtained token — no credentials are stored or transmitted:

client = BankingClient("client_id", "client_secret",
    token={"access_token": "...", "token_type": "Bearer"})

Or defer authentication to the first API call:

client = BankingClient("client_id", "client_secret", auto_auth=False)
# Token is acquired only when needed
balance = client.account_balance("76077736")

docs.title: Token Lifecycle
docs.text:
Clear a token from memory after use:

client.clear_token()

docs.title: Request Signing
docs.text:
For Banking and Agent clients, provide a signature_key to HMAC-SHA256 sign every deposit and withdrawal request:

client = BankingClient("id", "secret", signature_key="your-signing-key")
client.deposit(...)
# Automatically adds X-iHela-Signature header

For incoming webhooks or callbacks, verify signatures received from iHela:

from ihela_sdk import verify_signature

is_valid = verify_signature(payload, signature, secret_key)

docs.title: Error Handling Philosophy
docs.text:
The SDK raises a single generic iHelaAPIError for all gateway failures. The message is intentionally non-specific to prevent bots from mapping your environment (which accounts exist, which operations are live). Use the response_code attribute for internal programmatic handling only:

try:
    client.deposit(...)
except iHelaAPIError as e:
    # Never show response_code to end users
    logger.warning(f"Deposit failed with code {e.response_code}")

    if e.is_retryable:
        await retry_later(...)  # Transient failures (5xx, code "07")

    return {"error": "Transaction could not be completed"}

docs.title: Circuit Breaker
docs.text:
Prevents cascading failures when the iHela gateway is down. After 5 consecutive failures, all calls are suspended for 30 seconds:

client = BankingClient("id", "secret",
    circuit_breaker_threshold=5,     # failures before opening
    circuit_breaker_cooldown=30.0,   # seconds before retry
)

try:
    client.ping()
except iHelaCircuitOpenError:
    print("Gateway is down — circuit breaker open")

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
Prevent self-DoS with a token bucket per client instance:

client = BankingClient("id", "secret", rate_limit=100)  # 100 req/min

try:
    client.deposit(...)
except iHelaRateLimitError:
    print("Too many requests — slow down")

docs.title: Idempotency
docs.text:
On deposit and withdrawal operations, if no external_reference is provided, the SDK auto-generates a UUID to prevent duplicate transactions:

# Automatically gets a UUID external_reference
client.deposit(credit_account="ACT-123", credit_account_holder="John",
               amount=500.0, description="Payment", pin_code="1234")

# Or provide your own for idempotency across retries
client.deposit(credit_account="ACT-123", credit_account_holder="John",
               amount=500.0, description="Payment", pin_code="1234",
               external_reference="my-unique-ref-001")

docs.title: Async Usage
docs.text:
All clients have async counterparts. Use them in asyncio applications:

from ihela_sdk import AsyncBankingClient

client = AsyncBankingClient("client_id", "client_secret")

balance = await client.account_balance("76077736")
deposit = await client.deposit(
    credit_account="ACT-123",
    credit_account_holder="John Doe",
    amount=500.0,
    description="Invoice payment",
    external_reference="REF-001",
    pin_code="1234",
)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• See the API Reference for all available methods and schemas.
  • See Authentication for OAuth2 and SSO setup.
